best_edges.evidence: pullback_long is the largest contributor: 31 trades, $2415.3 PnL, 252.5 PnL/exposure hour, 100% win rate, median hold 12.0m. It has meaningful heat with worst MAE -403.2 and avg MAE -110.2, so expansion should not broaden weak-structure entries indiscriminately.

57 fast winners contributed $4884.8 with median MAE -29.75 and median hold 5.5m. Current objective quality is heavily dependent on these fast exits; broad entry-veto tightening risks damaging the main edge.

Only 5 trades, but $985.6 PnL, 197.1 avg PnL, 234.4 PnL/exposure hour, median MFE 146.2, no slow >=12h, worst MAE -346.8. Prior charm-wall admission geometry improved objective by +217 score and +$221 PnL; follow-through-only change was flat, so further tests need a new small admission dimension rather than repeating max distance.

best_edges.edge: squeeze_long clean continuation with controlled MAE
best_edges.evidence: 10 trades, $721 PnL, 187.2 PnL/exposure hour, worst MAE -208.25, median MFE 77.8, no slow trades. Prior squeeze TP/slope package from run_018 to run_021 was strongly accepted, but latest config already has high selected TP distances, so next work should seek cleaner admission or faster monetization rather than raw target extension.

VALID_1DTE_ONLY contributes 20 trades and $1305 PnL with no slow trades, but avg MAE -123.9 and PnL/exposure hour 168.8 trail the best 0DTE profiles. Prior direct 1DTE tightening hurt score and PnL, implying the edge is real but should be split, not tightened globally.
One trade, $91 PnL, 455 PnL/exposure hour, worst MAE -11.7, median MFE 131.3. The signal is promising but too under-sampled for aggressive tuning.

broken_edges.evidence: charm_pin_pullback_long contributes $1217.3 over 33 trades and has low avg MAE -74.4, but owns the global worst -527 MAE and low PnL/exposure hour 170.6. Static extended-VWAP filters overlap 17 protected extended-VWAP winners, so broad pruning would likely sacrifice edge.
broken_edges.problem: Tail-heavy profile with path-dependent failures hidden inside otherwise profitable fast charm-pin pullbacks.

broken_edges.evidence: Direct 1DTE quality gates previously reduced score by -594 and net profit by -$558.5. Current 1DTE PnL is material, so blunt structure/slope tightening is a broken approach.
broken_edges.problem: breakout_long_1dte needs a companion split rather than direct stricter thresholds.

broken_edges.evidence: STALE_0DTE fallback produced only 5 trades and $162.8 with poor efficiency versus VALID_0DTE; breakout_long_no_0dte STALE rows were especially inefficient at 40.5 PnL/exposure hour and avg MAE -210.6.
broken_edges.problem: No-0DTE stale fallback is low-throughput and low-efficiency; useful as insurance but not a profit-expansion target.

broken_edges.evidence: These active router-listed profiles are disabled or produced zero trades in the latest summary. There is no current evidence to optimize their local thresholds before a controlled activation/split test.
broken_edges.problem: Dead or unreachable in current router flow.

market_regime_read: Short sample, high-win-rate, take-profit-only regime over about 12.3 calendar days. Realized loss is 0% and slow >=12h trades are 0, so the main risk is hidden MAE and capital tied up before eventual profit. The current leader run_029 is only modestly ahead of repeated run_001-style configs, suggesting the optimizer is near a local plateau. Profit expansion should protect fast winners and profile-specific convex edges, not add broad filters. June was materially more efficient than July, and VALID_0DTE remains the dominant PnL source; 1DTE fallback is real but lower efficiency.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Separate 1DTE pullback/reclaim entries from breakout continuation without damaging breakout_long_1dte baseline.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Router ordering may cannibalize profitable breakout_long_1dte trades or the new split may be unreachable.
new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Profile
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_1DTE_ONLY entries with ES pullback/reclaim state, 1DTE option-map quality, MAE and hold-time distributions by matched route.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: 0DTE unavailable, 1DTE map valid, and ES state resembles pullback/reclaim rather than breakout continuation.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= current ~$1305, 1DTE avg MAE improves versus -123.9, total net profit >=98% of leader, and no total score degradation.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Isolate charm-pin entries that fail early progress while preserving clean high-extension winners.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Small-sample overfit or blocking protected extended-VWAP winners that resolve in under a few minutes.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Pin Early-Progress Tail Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Intratrade path features: first +1.5/+2.0 points time, MFE before MAE, first 15m MFE/MAE, profile, ES-VWAP extension, VWAP acceleration, pullback-from-high.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: charm_pin_pullback_long high-extension or recent-high-rejection entry that fails to show early positive progress after entry.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Reduce charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE toward <= -403 while retaining >=90% charm-pin PnL, >=95% protected fast extended-VWAP winners, total PnL >=98% of leader, and slow trades remain 0.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Route high charm-wall or pin-risk states into an existing dormant pin-avoid concept instead of globally vetoing charm_pin_pullback_long.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Dormant profile may be unreachable or may cannibalize clean charm-pin/charm-wall winners without improving risk-adjusted score.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Dormant Charm Wall Pin-Avoid Split Probe
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_0DTE charm/pin entries, route selection, standalone dormant profile stats, combined charm-pin/charm-wall MAE and protected fast-winner overlap.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE high charm-wall or pin-risk state currently routed into charm_pin_pullback_long or charm_wall_breakaway_long.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Activated dormant profile has positive standalone PnL or reduces combined charm/pin worst MAE while total net profit remains >=98% of leader and protected fast-winner count is not materially reduced.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Separate recoverable pullback heat from traps without weakening the pullback_long PnL engine.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Static config proxies may block profitable weak-structure pullbacks because current buckets show weak structure can still be profitable.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Pullback Deep-Heat Companion Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: pullback_long path features, weak-structure distances, VWAP acceleration, early MFE, MAE timing, and recovery time.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE pullback_long entry with weak structure distances, low or negative VWAP acceleration, no early MFE, and delayed recovery behavior.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Retain >=90% pullback_long PnL while improving pullback worst MAE from -403.2 and keeping total PnL/exposure hour flat or better.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Discover whether dormant delta/charm breakout can add non-overlapping continuation PnL.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: May overlap with squeeze_long or charm_wall_breakaway_long and dilute their cleaner efficiency.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Dormant Delta Charm Breakout Activation Probe
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_0DTE candidate route hits, option delta/charm pressure state, overlap with squeeze_long and charm_wall_breakaway_long, standalone PnL/exposure.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE continuation state with strong option pressure and charm/delta alignment but not already captured by active squeeze/charm-wall profiles.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Standalone PnL positive, total score no worse than -1%, PnL/exposure hour flat or better, worst MAE no worse than -527, and no material cannibalization of pullback_long or squeeze_long.

next_experiments.config_bias: Favor profile-local admission or monetization changes in squeeze_long only. Do not simply raise targets; preserve current option-map/order-book context and test whether cleaner slope/flow regimes improve PnL per exposure without extending holds.
next_experiments.goal: Increase squeeze_long contribution while preserving its relatively low tail MAE.
next_experiments.hypothesis: squeeze_long has enough MFE and controlled worst MAE to support selective expansion, but the next edge is likely better admission quality or faster realization rather than more target distance.
next_experiments.name: Squeeze Clean Continuation Efficiency Expansion
next_experiments.primary_metric: squeeze_long PnL, total PnL/exposure hour, squeeze worst MAE <= -225, and no increase in slow trades.
next_experiments.risk: Small sample; extra strictness can starve the profile, while looser admission may convert a clean continuation edge into slower heat.

next_experiments.config_bias: Test a small new charm-wall admission dimension while freezing exit compression and avoiding repetition of the flat max-distance-only follow-through. Keep require-breakout behavior intact.
next_experiments.goal: Find one or two more high-convexity charm-wall trades without degrading current profile efficiency.
next_experiments.hypothesis: Prior admission geometry helped, but repeating the same max-distance change was flat; a different small geometry axis may capture adjacent valid breakaway states.
next_experiments.name: Charm Wall Adjacent Geometry Expansion
next_experiments.primary_metric: charm_wall_breakaway_long PnL/exposure >=225, total net profit >=98% of leader, total worst MAE no worse than -527, and completed trades not down >10%.
next_experiments.risk: Only 5 current trades; a single added weak wall-chase can distort MAE or hold-time metrics.

next_experiments.config_bias: If legal, activate pullback_long_1dte as a companion in the one-dte router list while leaving breakout_long_1dte baseline thresholds largely intact. Treat this as route discovery, not direct 1DTE tightening.
next_experiments.goal: Improve 1DTE risk-adjusted efficiency through a split instead of damaging the existing 1DTE breakout fallback.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The 1DTE fallback is profitable but lower efficiency; prior direct tightening failed, so separating pullback/reclaim state may preserve PnL while lowering MAE.
next_experiments.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Router Split
next_experiments.primary_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL, 1DTE average MAE, total score, and total net profit retention >=98%.
next_experiments.risk: Router overlap can cannibalize current profitable breakout_long_1dte entries; reject if combined 1DTE PnL falls below current baseline.

next_experiments.config_bias: Use the already-enabled extended-VWAP rejection family only with very small boundary nudges, and explicitly check protected extended-VWAP winners. Avoid broad ES-VWAP max-distance or setup-quality floors.
next_experiments.goal: Trim the worst charm-pin/pullback MAE rows without materially reducing profit-expansion capacity.
next_experiments.hypothesis: All bad-MAE rows are covered by the extended-VWAP family or near misses, but protected fast extension winners make broad filtering unacceptable.
next_experiments.name: Protected Extended-VWAP Boundary Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: Worst MAE, retained total PnL >=98%, protected extended-VWAP winner retention >=90%, and total score.
next_experiments.risk: The near-miss set includes profitable trades; boundary expansion can block winners without removing the global worst if translated too broadly.

next_experiments.config_bias: Controlled dormant activation only: one router-list replacement plus minimal local enable/tune for the dormant pin-avoid concept. Do not optimize unrelated dormant profiles in the same run.
next_experiments.goal: Discover whether an existing pin-avoid route can absorb charm/pin tail states while leaving current winners intact.
next_experiments.hypothesis: charm_pin_pullback_long is profitable but tail-heavy; a dormant split may isolate unfavorable pin/charm states better than global vetoing.
next_experiments.name: Dormant Charm Pin Avoid Split Probe
next_experiments.primary_metric: Standalone dormant profile PnL, combined charm/pin worst MAE, protected fast-winner retention, and total score.
next_experiments.risk: Profile may be unreachable or may cannibalize the profitable charm_pin_pullback_long fast-winner population.

next_experiments.config_bias: Keep stale-fallback changes isolated to no-0DTE profiles and avoid touching VALID_0DTE. Favor efficiency validation over profit expansion here.
next_experiments.goal: Prevent low-efficiency stale fallback exposure from dragging risk-adjusted score if future samples include more stale data.
next_experiments.hypothesis: STALE_0DTE fallback has low PnL/exposure and some heat; it is not the growth engine but can hurt robustness.
next_experiments.name: Stale No-0DTE Fallback Efficiency Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: Fallback PnL/exposure, fallback worst MAE, unchanged VALID_0DTE PnL, and total PnL retention >=98%.
next_experiments.risk: Only 5 stale trades; overfitting this state could remove useful insurance for rare data outages.

research_notes: Do not emit direct config changes from this plan. The evidence supports protecting the current leader rather than broad throughput expansion: fast winners and pullback_long are the base edge, while squeeze_long and charm_wall_breakaway_long are the best profit-expansion candidates. The biggest avoidable mistake is broad static pruning of extended-VWAP or setup-quality states: a setup floor near 62 would remove the worst tail but retain only 44.8% PnL, and protected extended-VWAP winners are a real contributor. Prior memory also says direct 1DTE tightening and call-wall broadening hurt; prefer splits or isolated activation tests. Repeating already-flat charm-wall max-distance-only follow-through has low value.
risk_controls: Reject experiments that reduce total net profit below 98% of leader unless worst MAE improves dramatically and protected fast-winner retention is high.
Track protected fast_winners, low_mae_winners, high_pnl_winners, and extended_vwap_winners explicitly for every filter-like experiment.
Keep slow >=12h trades at 0; any reappearance is an immediate failure for this no-stop-loss strategy.
Do not use broad setup-quality, ES-VWAP distance, or ES-structure pruning unless the retained PnL and protected-winner reports are favorable.
Separate VALID_0DTE, VALID_1DTE_ONLY, STALE_0DTE, and EXPIRED_0DTE metrics; do not let stale fallback improvements mask damage to VALID_0DTE.
For dormant activation, test only one dormant concept at a time and include the required router-list replacement as one logical experiment family.
Treat all one-trade profile results, especially call_wall_acceptance_long and EXPIRED_0DTE fallback, as discovery signals rather than proven edges.
